question: What is an exposure in dbt and when do you use it?
answer: An exposure is a YAML declaration of a downstream artifact that consumes dbt models: dashboards, ML models, APIs, or applications. Define it in schema.yml with the type, owner, depends_on (list of ref() models), and URL. Exposures make lineage end-to-end visible in dbt docs and Explorer: you can see exactly which BI reports depend on which models. The gotcha: exposures do not automate anything; they are metadata only. Their value is blast-radius analysis: before renaming a column, see which dashboards will break.
